title: How do we use YouTube’s latest AI feature?#tutorialyoutube #tutorial #youtube #shorts
description:
🚀 YouTube Shorts New AI Tools! 

.
.

YouTube just dropped a massive update for Shorts creators! In this video, we explore the new generative AI tools powered by Google DeepMind's Veo. Creating high-quality content is now easier than ever.
Key Features:
• AI Playground: Your new home for gen AI creation with pre-filled prompts.
• Dream it, Prompt it (Veo): Generate cinematic video clips from text or reference photos.
• Edit with AI: Automatically transform your raw footage into a polished first draft.
• Photo to Video: Bring your still gallery photos to life instantly.

@YouTube @youtubecreators

analysis.hook_text: Screen recording of YouTube app home screen, a hand icon immediately points to the search icon.
analysis.hook_analysis: This hook works by immediately demonstrating the first step of a new, potentially unknown feature, creating instant curiosity and positioning the creator as an expert.

structure_breakdown.content: Screen recording of YouTube app. A hand pointer immediately moves towards the search icon.
User types 'AI' into the search bar, demonstrating searching for AI-related content.
A video plays; the pointer highlights the comments section, revealing a 'New AI' feature icon (star with sparkles).
Tapping the AI icon reveals AI-generated questions about the video content, and the pointer scrolls through them.
Tapping one of the AI-generated questions makes the video automatically jump to the relevant timestamp, demonstrating the feature's utility.

analysis.replicability_notes: This format is highly replicable using just a mobile phone for screen recording and a simple voiceover. No complex editing or expensive equipment is required.

title: How To Make Viral Skeleton Educational Shorts #shorts #viral #viralshorts
description:
These skeleton “what if” animations are going viral… and I recreated them straight from my phone using AI 👀💀

Want the full step-by-step breakdown + all the prompts and reference images?
Comment AI and I’ll send you the full video 🔥

#shorts #animation #viral #ai #skeleton #viralshort #viralshorts

analysis.hook_text: Here's how I recreated these insane skeleton animations using only my phone.
analysis.hook_analysis: This hook immediately showcases an impressive, trending result and promises a simple, accessible method, creating curiosity and setting high expectations for the tutorial.

structure_breakdown.content: Creator introduces the topic, promising to show how to create viral skeleton animations using only a phone, while showing examples of the animations.
Explains the popularity and virality (millions of views) of these skeleton videos, emphasizing their simple creation with 'only two tools'.
First instruction: Open ChatGPT, go to GPTs, search for 'skeleton shorts', and start a chat.
Instruction to type a wild idea (e.g., 'drinking raw eggs') into ChatGPT, showing it instantly generating six detailed image prompts. Emphasizes copying one prompt.
Instruction to head to Leonardo AI, paste the prompt, upload a reference image, and generate, showcasing the resulting 'super clean skeleton visuals'.
Instruction for OpenArt: click video, upload the image, describe the desired motion (e.g., 'skeleton drinking raw eggs at the gym'), generate, and then shows the final smooth skeleton animation.
Specific call to action: 'comment AI and I'll send you the full video with the reference'.

analysis.cta_text: comment AI and I'll send you the full video with the reference
analysis.replicability_notes: A solo creator can easily replicate this with a smartphone, screen recording software, and basic video editing skills. The AI tools demonstrated are also readily accessible.
